content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
• Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
December 15, 1998
Michael Alvarez
City Councilman
City of Orange
300 E. Chapman Avenue
Orange, California 92666-1591
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-98-293
Dear Mr. Alvarez:
We have received your letter ofNovember 9, 1998. To the extent that your letter is a
request for advice about the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)
1
, we must decline to advise you on
these matters for two reasons. First, the events described in your letter have afready taken place.
By law, we may not advise about such past conduct. Commission advice is prospective only.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Second, your inquiry pertains to the duties of another person
under the Act, not your own. By law, we may not advise you about another person’s duties
under the Act, unless you have been authorized by that person to seek advice on his or her behalf.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).)
Ifyou believe that a violation of the Act has occurred, you may contact the Commission’s
Enforcement Division to discuss filing a complaint.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
ena Cçfunsel //
By:/
John Vrgelli
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SOC :JV:tls
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Comrnissionregulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
1:\Advice Ltrs\98293.wpd

sections.question_synthetic: The requester (Michael Alvarez, City Councilman) sought FPPC advice regarding matters under the Political Reform Act, though the specific details of his inquiry are not provided in the response letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice for two reasons: (1) the events described had already occurred and FPPC advice is prospective only, and (2) the inquiry pertained to another person's duties under the Act rather than the requester's own duties, and no authorization from that person was provided. The requester was directed to contact the Enforcement Division if they believed a violation had occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The substantive inquiry details are not provided in this response document itself. The letter cites Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (B) as the basis for declining.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester (Michael Alvarez, City Councilman) sought FPPC advice regarding matters under the Political Reform Act, though the specific details of his inquiry are not provided in the response letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice for two reasons: (1) the events described had already occurred and FPPC advice is prospective only, and (2) the inquiry pertained to another person's duties under the Act rather than the requester's own duties, and no authorization from that person was provided. The requester was directed to contact the Enforcement Division if they believed a violation had occurred.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined Mr. Alvarez's request for advice on two grounds: the matters involved past conduct (for which prospective advice only is available) and concerned another person's duties under the Political Reform Act without that person's authorization.
